page: Amazon Web Services r8idb.4xlarge is a memory-optimized server instance built on the x86_64 architecture. It features a dedicated Intel processor running at a clock speed of 3.9 GHz, configured with 8 physical cores and 16 virtual CPUs (vCPUs) via 2.0 threads per core. The instance is managed by the AWS Nitro hypervisor, ensuring dedicated resource allocation.

This server type is equipped with 128.0 GB of system memory, yielding a memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU. Storage is bundled directly with the instance, consisting of a 950 GB local NVMe SSD instance store volume. It does not include any graphics processing units (GPUs) or complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

The network configuration provides a baseline bandwidth of 17 Gbps. From a cost-efficiency perspective, the r8idb.4xlarge offers a high density of memory and local NVMe storage relative to its 16 vCPUs, making it a specialized option for workloads requiring fast local disk access and large memory footprints without the overhead of external network-attached storage.

Due to its hardware profile, this instance is designed for memory-intensive applications, database hosting, caching layers, and data processing tasks that benefit from high-speed local NVMe instance storage and a high memory-to-CPU ratio.
description: Amazon Web Services r8idb.4xlarge is a memory-optimized server instance powered by a dedicated 3.9 GHz Intel processor. Operating on the x86_64 architecture under the Nitro hypervisor, it provides 16 vCPUs across 8 physical cores with 2.0 threads per core. The hardware configuration includes 128.0 GB of system memory, yielding 8.0 GB per core, and a bundled 950 GB local NVMe SSD instance store volume. It features a baseline network bandwidth of 17 Gbps and does not contain any GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses. This instance delivers high resource density for memory-intensive applications and local storage-dependent workloads. It is suited for database hosting, caching layers, and memory-heavy data processing tasks. The inclusion of high-speed local NVMe storage provides qualitative cost efficiency by eliminating the need for external network-attached storage for high-throughput database operations.
